@article{doi101016jpalaeo201611005,
    author = "Bond, David P.G. and Grasby, Stephen E.",
    title = "On the causes of mass extinctions",
    year = "2016",
    journal = "Palaeogeography Palaeoclimatology Palaeoecology",
    abstract = "The temporal link between large igneous province (LIP) eruptions and at least half of the major extinctions of the Phanerozoic implies that large scale volcanism is the main driver of mass extinction. Here we review almost twenty biotic crises between the early Cambrian and end Cretaceous and explore potential causal mechanisms. Most extinctions are associated with global warming and proximal killers such as marine anoxia (including the Early/Middle Cambrian, the Late Ordovician, the intra-Silurian, intra-Devonian, end-Permian, and Early Jurassic crises). Many, but not all of these are accompanied by large negative carbon isotope excursions, supporting a volcanogenic origin. Most post-Silurian biocrises affected both terrestrial and marine biospheres, suggesting that atmospheric processes were crucial in driving global extinctions. Volcanogenic-atmospheric kill mechanisms include ocean acidification, toxic metal poisoning, acid rain, and ozone damage and consequent increased UV-B radiation, volcanic darkness, cooling and photosynthetic shutdown, each of which has been implicated in numerous events. Intriguingly, some of the most voluminous LIPs such as the oceanic plateaus of the Cretaceous were emplaced with minimal faunal losses and so volume of magma is not the only factor governing LIP lethality. The missing link might be continental configuration because the best examples of the LIP/extinction relationship occurred during the time of Pangaea. Many of the proximal kill mechanisms in LIP/extinction scenarios are also potential effects of bolide impact, including cooling, warming, acidification and ozone destruction. However, the absence of convincing temporal links between impacts and extinctions other than the Chicxulub-Cretaceous example, suggests that impacts are not the main driver of extinctions. With numerous competing extinction scenarios, and the realisation that some of the purported environmental stresses may once again be driving mass extinction, we explore how experimental biology might inform our understanding of ancient extinctions as well as future crises.",

53. Penn, Justin L. and Deutsch, Curtis and Payne, Jonathan L. and Sperling, Erik A., 2018, Temperature-dependent hypoxia explains biogeography and severity of end-Permian marine mass extinction: Science.

DOI: 10.1126/science.aat1327

Abstract

Rapid climate change at the end of the Permian Period (~252 million years ago) is the hypothesized trigger for the largest mass extinction in Earth's history. We present model simulations of the Permian/Triassic climate transition that reproduce the ocean warming and oxygen (O 2) loss indicated by the geologic record. The effect of these changes on animal survival is evaluated using the Metabolic Index (Φ), a measure of scope for aerobic activity governed by organismal traits sampled in diverse modern species. Modeled loss of aerobic habitat predicts lower extinction intensity in the tropics, a pattern confirmed with a spatially explicit analysis of the marine fossil record. The combined physiological stresses of ocean warming and O 2 loss can account for more than half the magnitude of the "Great Dying."

BibTeX

    year = "2018",
    journal = "Science",
    abstract = {Rapid climate change at the end of the Permian Period (\textasciitilde 252 million years ago) is the hypothesized trigger for the largest mass extinction in Earth's history. We present model simulations of the Permian/Triassic climate transition that reproduce the ocean warming and oxygen (O 2) loss indicated by the geologic record. The effect of these changes on animal survival is evaluated using the Metabolic Index (Φ), a measure of scope for aerobic activity governed by organismal traits sampled in diverse modern species. Modeled loss of aerobic habitat predicts lower extinction intensity in the tropics, a pattern confirmed with a spatially explicit analysis of the marine fossil record. The combined physiological stresses of ocean warming and O 2 loss can account for more than half the magnitude of the "Great Dying."},

54. Fielding, Christopher R. and Frank, Tracy D. and McLoughlin, Stephen and Vajda, Vivi and Mays, Chris and Tevyaw, Allen P. and Winguth, A. and Winguth, C. and Nicoll, Robert S. and Bocking, Malcolm and Crowley, James L., 2019, Age and pattern of the southern high-latitude continental end-Permian extinction constrained by multiproxy analysis: Nature Communications.

DOI: 10.1038/s41467-018-07934-z

Abstract

Past studies of the end-Permian extinction (EPE), the largest biotic crisis of the Phanerozoic, have not resolved the timing of events in southern high-latitudes. Here we use palynology coupled with high-precision CA-ID-TIMS dating of euhedral zircons from continental sequences of the Sydney Basin, Australia, to show that the collapse of the austral Permian Glossopteris flora occurred prior to 252.3 Ma (~370 kyrs before the main marine extinction). Weathering proxies indicate that floristic changes occurred during a brief climate perturbation in a regional alluvial landscape that otherwise experienced insubstantial change in fluvial style, insignificant reorganization of the depositional surface, and no abrupt aridification. Palaeoclimate modelling suggests a moderate shift to warmer summer temperatures and amplified seasonality in temperature across the EPE, and warmer and wetter conditions for all seasons into the Early Triassic. The terrestrial EPE and a succeeding peak in Ni concentration in the Sydney Basin correlate, respectively, to the onset of the primary extrusive and intrusive phases of the Siberian Traps Large Igneous Province.

55. Bush, Andrew M. and Wang, Steve C. and Payne, J. and Heim, N., 2020, A framework for the integrated analysis of the magnitude, selectivity, and biotic effects of extinction and origination: Paleobiology: v. 46, no. 1: p. 1-22.

DOI: 10.1017/pab.2019.35 Source

Abstract

Abstract. The taxonomic and ecologic composition of Earth's biota has shifted dramatically through geologic time, with some clades going extinct while others diversified. Here, we derive a metric that quantifies the change in biotic composition due to extinction or origination and show that it equals the product of extinction/origination magnitude and selectivity (variation in magnitude among groups). We also define metrics that describe the extent to which a recovery (1) reinforced or reversed the effects of extinction on biotic composition and (2) changed composition in ways uncorrelated with the extinction. To demonstrate the approach, we analyzed an updated compilation of stratigraphic ranges of marine animal genera. We show that mass extinctions were not more selective than background intervals at the phylum level; rather, they tended to drive greater taxonomic change due to their higher magnitudes. Mass extinctions did not represent a separate class of events with respect to either strength of selectivity or effect. Similar observations apply to origination during recoveries from mass extinctions, and on average, extinction and origination were similarly selective and drove similar amounts of biotic change. Elevated origination during recoveries drove bursts of compositional change that varied considerably in effect. In some cases, origination partially reversed the effects of extinction, returning the biota toward the pre-extinction composition; in others, it reinforced the effects of the extinction, magnifying biotic change. Recoveries were as important as extinction events in shaping the marine biota, and their selectivity deserves systematic study alongside that of extinction.

56. Davis, William J., 2023, Mass Extinctions and Their Relationship With Atmospheric Carbon Dioxide Concentration: Implications for Earth's Future: Earth s Future.

DOI: 10.1029/2022ef003336

Abstract

Abstract Industrialization has raised the concentration of carbon dioxide (CO 2) in Earth's atmosphere by half since 1770, posing a risk from ocean acidification to global biodiversity, including phytoplankton that synthesize approximately (∼) 50% of planetary oxygen. This risk is estimated here from the fossil record and implications for our energy and economic future are explored. Over the last 534 million years (Myr), 50 extinction events present as peaks of genus loss‐and‐recovery cycles, each spanning ∼3–40 Myr. Atmospheric CO 2 concentration oscillates with percent genus loss, leading in phase by ∼4 Myr and sharing harmonic periodicities at ∼10, 26 and 63 Myr. Over the last 210 Myr, where data resolution is highest, biodiversity loss is correlated with atmospheric CO 2 concentration, but not with long‐term global temperature nor with marginal radiative forcing of temperature by atmospheric CO 2. The end‐Cretaceous extinction of the dinosaurs is anomalous, occurring during a 20‐million year depression in atmospheric CO 2 concentration and rising global temperature. Today's atmospheric CO 2 concentration, ∼421 parts per million by volume (ppmv), corresponds in the most recent marine fossil record to a biodiversity loss of 6.39%, implying that contemporary anthropogenic CO 2 emissions are killing ocean life now. The United Nations Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change projects that unabated fossil fuel use could elevate atmospheric CO 2 concentration to 800 ppmv by 2100, approaching the 870 ppmv mean concentration of the last 19 natural extinction events. Reversing this first global anthropogenic mass extinction requires reducing net anthropogenic CO 2 emissions to zero, optimally by 2% per year starting immediately.

57. Monarrez, Pedro M. and Heim, N. and Payne, J., 2023, Reduced strength and increased variability of extinction selectivity during mass extinctions: Royal Society Open Science: v. 10, no. 9.

DOI: 10.1098/rsos.230795

Abstract

Two of the traits most often observed to correlate with extinction risk in marine animals are geographical range and body size. However, the relative effects of these two traits on extinction risk have not been investigated systematically for either background times or during mass extinctions. To close this knowledge gap, we measure and compare extinction selectivity of geographical range and body size of genera within five classes of benthic marine animals across the Phanerozoic using capture–mark–recapture models. During background intervals, narrow geographical range is strongly associated with greater extinction probability, whereas smaller body size is more weakly associated with greater extinction probability. During mass extinctions, the association between geographical range and extinction probability is reduced in every class and fully eliminated in some, whereas the association between body size and extinction probability varies in strength and direction across classes. While geographical range is universally the stronger predictor of survival during background intervals, variation among classes during mass extinction suggests a fundamental shift in extinction processes during these global catastrophes.

58. Song, Haijun and Wu, Yuyang and Dai, Xu and Corso, Jacopo Dal and Wang, Fengyu and Feng, Yan and Chu, Daoliang and Tian, Li and Song, Huyue and Foster, William J., 2023, Respiratory protein-driven selectivity during the Permian–Triassic mass extinction: Zenodo.

DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.8079148 Source

Abstract

<strong>Fossil occurrence data</strong> Fossil data used to calculate diversity variation were obtained from a previously published database of Permian‒Triassic marine fossils (Song et al., 2018; Song et al., 2020). The database contains 52,322 occurrences at the generic level from 1,768 published papers and the Paleobiology Database, spanning the Late Permian Changhsingian to the Late Triassic Rhaetian (Data 1). Our analysis is based on the occurrences of genera, as taphonomy prevents species-level identifications. Within the considered interval, a total of 1,097 genera belong to 13 major clades, including two clades of protozoa (foraminifera and radiolarians), nine clades of invertebrates (corals, sponges, brachiopods, bryozoans, ostracods, cephalopods, gastropods, bivalves, and echinoderms), and two clades of vertebrates (conodonts and fishes). For marine arthropods, we used only ostracod data because ostracods are abundant in the fossil record during the late Permian. Other marine arthropods are very rare in this time interval. For example, only two genera of trilobite, one genus of chelicera, and one genus of decapod are recorded in the Changhsingian bin compared to &gt; 100 genera of ostracods in the Paleobiology Database. We did not consider background extinction in the Late Permian because many studies have shown that the background extinction rate of marine taxa in the Changhsingian is negligible compared to the mass extinction interval around the Permian‒Triassic boundary (Yin et al., 2007; Shen et al., 2011; Song et al., 2013; Fan et al., 2020). Therefore, the results using the Changhsingian and Induan fossil data reflect a selectivity pattern of the Permian‒Triassic mass extinction rather than background extinction. <strong>Body size data</strong> We used the comprehensive database of Schaal et al. (2016) to assign body size expressed as the maximum length for each species. Using the maximum size for each taxon is a common approach for body size studies, as the effects of juvenile specimens in the database can be avoided (Stanley, 1973; Jablonski, 1997; Lockwood, 2005; Heim et al., 2015; Payne et al., 2016; Schaal et al., 2016). We followed the same methods to compile additional data for taxa not included in this database. A number of recently published databases were used to compile the size data, including references (Romano et al., 2016; Shi et al., 2016; Foster et al., 2018; Chen et al., 2019; Feng et al., 2020; Foster et al., 2020). Other size data were mainly obtained from the published taxonomic literature (see Data 2). Only common taxa from both Changhsingian and Induan are included because these taxa have abundant fossil data to study their size change during the Permian-Triassic interval, i.e., foraminifera, brachiopods, ostracods, gastropods, cephalopods, bivalves, conodonts, and fishes. Other taxa including corals, sponges, radiolarians, bryozoans, and echinoderms are absent/very rare in the Induan bin (see Data 1), and accordingly are not included in this study. The Changhsingian and Induan body size dataset is composed of 1495 species in 635 genera belonging to eight common clades. Other data were obtained from the above fossil occurrence and body size datasets. <strong>References</strong> Chen, J., Song, H., He, W., Tong, J., Wang, F., Wu, S., 2019. Size variation of brachiopods from the Late Permian through the Middle Triassic in South China: Evidence for the Lilliput Effect following the Permian-Triassic extinction. Palaeogeography, Palaeoclimatology, Palaeoecology, 519: 248–257. Fan, J.-x., Shen, S.-z., Erwin, D.H., Sadler, P.M., MacLeod, N., Cheng, Q.-m., Hou, X.-d., Yang, J., Wang, X.-d., Wang, Y., 2020. A high-resolution summary of Cambrian to Early Triassic marine invertebrate biodiversity. Science, 367(6475): 272–277. Feng, Y., Song, H., Bond, D.P.G., 2020. Size variations in foraminifers from the early Permian to the Late Triassic: implications for the Guadalupian–Lopingian and the Permian–Triassic mass extinctions. Paleobiology, 46(4): 511–532. Evolutionary and ecophenotypic controls on bivalve body size distributions following the end-Permian mass extinction. Global and Planetary Change, 185: 103088. Foster, W., Lehrmann, D., Yu, M., Ji, L., Martindale, R., 2018. Persistent environmental stress delayed the recovery of marine communities in the aftermath of the latest Permian mass extinction. Paleoceanography and Paleoclimatology, 33(4): 338–353. Heim, N.A., Knope, M.L., Schaal, E.K., Wang, S.C., Payne, J.L., 2015. Cope's rule in the evolution of marine animals. Science, 347(6224): 867–870. Jablonski, D., 1997. Body-size evolution in Cretaceous molluscs and the status of Cope's rule. Nature, 385(6613): 250–252. Lockwood, R., 2005. Body size, extinction events, and the early Cenozoic record of veneroid bivalves: a new role for recoveries? Paleobiology, 31(4): 578–590. Romano, C., Koot, M.B., Kogan, I., Brayard, A., Minikh, A.V., Brinkmann, W., Bucher, H., Kriwet, J., 2016. Permian–Triassic Osteichthyes (bony fishes): diversity dynamics and body size evolution. Biological Reviews, 91(1): 106–147. Schaal, E.K., Clapham, M.E., Rego, B.L., Wang, S.C., Payne, J.L., 2016. Comparative size evolution of marine clades from the Late Permian through Middle Triassic.     publisher = "Zenodo",
    abstract = "<strong>Fossil occurrence data</strong> Fossil data used to calculate diversity variation were obtained from a previously published database of Permian‒Triassic marine fossils (Song et al., 2018; Song et al., 2020). The database contains 52,322 occurrences at the generic level from 1,768 published papers and the Paleobiology Database, spanning the Late Permian Changhsingian to the Late Triassic Rhaetian (Data 1). Our analysis is based on the occurrences of genera, as taphonomy prevents species-level identifications. Within the considered interval, a total of 1,097 genera belong to 13 major clades, including two clades of protozoa (foraminifera and radiolarians), nine clades of invertebrates (corals, sponges, brachiopods, bryozoans, ostracods, cephalopods, gastropods, bivalves, and echinoderms), and two clades of vertebrates (conodonts and fishes). For marine arthropods, we used only ostracod data because ostracods are abundant in the fossil record during the late Permian. Other marine arthropods are very rare in this time interval. For example, only two genera of trilobite, one genus of chelicera, and one genus of decapod are recorded in the Changhsingian bin compared to \&gt; 100 genera of ostracods in the Paleobiology Database. We did not consider background extinction in the Late Permian because many studies have shown that the background extinction rate of marine taxa in the Changhsingian is negligible compared to the mass extinction interval around the Permian‒Triassic boundary (Yin et al., 2007; Shen et al., 2011; Song et al., 2013; Fan et al., 2020). Therefore, the results using the Changhsingian and Induan fossil data reflect a selectivity pattern of the Permian‒Triassic mass extinction rather than background extinction. <strong>Body size data</strong> We used the comprehensive database of Schaal et al. (2016) to assign body size expressed as the maximum length for each species. Using the maximum size for each taxon is a common approach for body size studies, as the effects of juvenile specimens in the database can be avoided (Stanley, 1973; Jablonski, 1997; Lockwood, 2005; Heim et al., 2015; Payne et al., 2016; Schaal et al., 2016). We followed the same methods to compile additional data for taxa not included in this database. A number of recently published databases were used to compile the size data, including references (Romano et al., 2016; Shi et al., 2016; Foster et al., 2018; Chen et al., 2019; Feng et al., 2020; Foster et al., 2020). Other size data were mainly obtained from the published taxonomic literature (see Data 2). Only common taxa from both Changhsingian and Induan are included because these taxa have abundant fossil data to study their size change during the Permian-Triassic interval, i.e., foraminifera, brachiopods, ostracods, gastropods, cephalopods, bivalves, conodonts, and fishes. Other taxa including corals, sponges, radiolarians, bryozoans, and echinoderms are absent/very rare in the Induan bin (see Data 1), and accordingly are not included in this study. The Changhsingian and Induan body size dataset is composed of 1495 species in 635 genera belonging to eight common clades. Other data were obtained from the above fossil occurrence and body size datasets. <strong>References</strong> Chen, J., Song, H., He, W., Tong, J., Wang, F., Wu, S., 2019. Size variation of brachiopods from the Late Permian through the Middle Triassic in South China: Evidence for the Lilliput Effect following the Permian-Triassic extinction. Palaeogeography, Palaeoclimatology, Palaeoecology, 519: 248–257. Fan, J.-x., Shen, S.-z., Erwin, D.H., Sadler, P.M., MacLeod, N., Cheng, Q.-m., Hou, X.-d., Yang, J., Wang, X.-d., Wang, Y., 2020. A high-resolution summary of Cambrian to Early Triassic marine invertebrate biodiversity. Science, 367(6475): 272–277. Feng, Y., Song, H., Bond, D.P.G., 2020. Size variations in foraminifers from the early Permian to the Late Triassic: implications for the Guadalupian–Lopingian and the Permian–Triassic mass extinctions. Paleobiology, 46(4): 511–532. Foster, W., Gliwa, J., Lembke, C., Pugh, A., Hofmann, R., Tietje, M., Varela, S., Foster, L., Korn, D., Aberhan, M., 2020. Evolutionary and ecophenotypic controls on bivalve body size distributions following the end-Permian mass extinction. Global and Planetary Change, 185: 103088. Foster, W., Lehrmann, D., Yu, M., Ji, L., Martindale, R., 2018. Persistent environmental stress delayed the recovery of marine communities in the aftermath of the latest Permian mass extinction. Paleoceanography and Paleoclimatology, 33(4): 338–353. Heim, N.A., Knope, M.L., Schaal, E.K., Wang, S.C., Payne, J.L., 2015. Cope's rule in the evolution of marine animals. Science, 347(6224): 867–870. Jablonski, D., 1997. Body-size evolution in Cretaceous molluscs and the status of Cope's rule. Nature, 385(6613): 250–252. Lockwood, R., 2005. Body size, extinction events, and the early Cenozoic record of veneroid bivalves: a new role for recoveries? Paleobiology, 31(4): 578–590. Payne, J.L., Bush, A.M., Heim, N.A., Knope, M.L., McCauley, D.J., 2016. Ecological selectivity of the emerging mass extinction in the oceans. Science, 353(6305): 1284–1286. Romano, C., Koot, M.B., Kogan, I., Brayard, A., Minikh, A.V., Brinkmann, W., Bucher, H., Kriwet, J., 2016. Permian–Triassic Osteichthyes (bony fishes): diversity dynamics and body size evolution. Biological Reviews, 91(1): 106–147. Schaal, E.K., Clapham, M.E., Rego, B.L., Wang, S.C., Payne, J.L., 2016. Comparative size evolution of marine clades from the Late Permian through Middle Triassic. Paleobiology, 42(1): 127–142. Shen, S., Crowley, J.L., Wang, Y., Bowring, S.A., Erwin, D.H., Sadler, P.M., Cao, C., Rothman, D.H., Henderson, C.M., Ramezani, J., Zhang, H., Shen, Y., Wang, X., Wang, W., Mu, L., Li, W., Tang, Y., Liu, X., Liu, L., Zeng, Y., Jiang, Y., Jin, Y., 2011. Calibrating the end-Permian mass extinction. Science, 334(6061): 1367–1372. Shi, G.R., Zhang, Y.-c., Shen, S.-z., He, W.-h., 2016. Song, H., Huang, S., Jia, E., Dai, X., Wignall, P.B., Dunhill, A.M., 2020. Flat latitudinal diversity gradient caused by the Permian–Triassic mass extinction. Science Advances, 4(10): eaat5091. Song, H., Wignall, P.B., Tong, J., Yin, H., 2013. Two pulses of extinction during the Permian-Triassic crisis. Nature Geoscience, 6(1): 52–56. Stanley, S.M., 1973. An explanation for Cope's rule. Evolution, 27(1): 1–26. Yin, H., Feng, Q., Lai, X., Baud, A., Tong, J., 2007. The protracted Permo-Triassic crisis and multi-episode extinction around the Permian-Triassic boundary. Global and Planetary Change, 55(1–3): 1–20.",
    url = "https://zenodo.org/record/8079148",
    doi = "10.5281/zenodo.8079148"
}

59. Domingos, L. and da Silva Júnior, Antonio Furtado and Diniz, C. and Rosa, J. and Terzian, A. L. B. and Resstel, Leonardo Barbosa Moraes, 2024, P2X7 receptors modulate acquisition of cue fear extinction and contextual background memory generalization in male mice.: Neuropharmacology: v. 261: p. 110177.

DOI: 10.1016/j.neuropharm.2024.110177 Source

Abstract

The purinergic P2X7 receptors (P2X7R) are activated by adenosine triphosphate (ATP) in several brain regions, particularly those involved with emotional control and the regulation of fear-related memories. Here, we investigate the role of P2X7R in fear learning memory, specifically in the acquisition and consolidation phases of the cued fear conditioning paradigm. C57Bl/6 wildtype (WT) male mice that received a single i.p. injection of the selective P2X7R antagonist A438079 prior the conditioning session showed generalization of cued fear memory and impaired fear extinction recall in the test session, while those treated prior the extinction session exhibited a similar behavior profile accompanied by resistance in the extinction learning. However, no effects were observed when this drug was administered immediately after the conditioning, extinction, or before the test session. Our results with P2X7R knockout (P2X7 KO) mice showed a behavioral profile that mirrored the collective effects observed across all pharmacological treatment conditions. This suggests that the P2X7R KO model effectively replicates the behavioral changes induced by the pharmacological interventions, demonstrating that we have successfully isolated the role of P2X7R in the fear and extinction phases of memory. These findings highlight the role of P2X7R in the acquisition and recall of extinction memory and supports P2X7R as a promising candidate for controlling abnormal fear processing, with potential applications for stress exposure-related disorders such as post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD).

61. Martínez‐Rubio, Natàlia and Sayol, F. and Lapiedra, O., 2024, Extinction selectivity obscures patterns of trait‐dependent endangerment in Columbiformes: Global Ecology and Biogeography: v. 33, no. 7.

DOI: 10.1111/geb.13851 Source

Abstract

Understanding how extinction has occurred in the recent past is crucial to unravel its main drivers as well as to implement effective conservation practices to minimize global biodiversity loss. It has long been hypothesized that extinction risk is not randomly distributed among traits of species. However, the actual traits making species more prone to extinction may have been overlooked because already extinct species are often not considered in comparative analyses of extinction risk. We characterized the drivers of extinction in a cosmopolitan bird clade, including Holocene and contemporary extinctions potentially related to human impacts and provided evidence of an ‘extinction selectivity’ in species traits.Global.Anthropocene.Columbiformes clade, pigeons and doves.We constructed a new phylogenetic hypothesis of the Columbiformes, a cosmopolitan bird clade consisting of 33 recently extinct and 351 extant species. Then, we integrated data on geography, behaviour and morphology to reveal the drivers of extinction risk. We used phylogenetic generalized least square models to test the effect of geography, behaviour and morphology in the risk of extinction and identified differences in the drivers of extinction when including versus excluding recently extinct species.Our analysis revealed that Columbiformes endemic to islands with ground‐foraging habits, weak flying abilities, migratory behaviour and larger body sizes are more vulnerable to extinction. Our results also show that excluding recently extinct species identifies extinction drivers different from those when including recently extinct species.Only by accurately identifying the traits that increase extinction risk we can develop targeted conservation measures that promote the long‐term persistence of threatened species. Extinction selectivity has important implications for the conservation of biological communities and ultimately ecosystem functioning, considering the critical role Columbiformes often play as seed dispersers.

62. Heyn, Sara A and DiMaio, Samantha and Herringa, Ryan J, 2026, Neurobiological Signatures of Dyadic Transmission of Fear Extinction in Adolescent Trauma Exposure and Posttraumatic Stress.: Biological psychiatry global open science.

DOI: 10.1016/j.bpsgos.2026.100714 Source

Abstract

BACKGROUND: The use of social cues to attenuate prior learned fear associations is a critical skill for normative development. Impairments in extinction learning underlie emerging models of posttraumatic stress disorder (PTSD) in adults. However, few have investigated how trauma and/or related psychopathology may impact the unique neural and physiological processes of observed-parent vicarious safety learning during adolescence. METHODS: This study enrolled typically developing (TD) (n = 42) and trauma-exposed (TE) (n = 45) parent-child dyads, where youths were classified as having low (TE+low) or high (TE+high) posttraumatic stress symptoms (PTSSs). Dyads completed acquisition (day 1), direct and vicarious extinction by watching a video of parent direct (day 2), and extinction recall (day 3). Youths completed all phases during functional magnetic resonance imaging. Analysis of behavioral outcomes, parent-child autonomic synchrony, and whole-brain voxelwise activation were used to characterize trauma- and PTSS-related correlates of vicarious extinction. RESULTS: Autonomic synchrony analyses suggest that TE youths did not exhibit normative vicarious extinction transmission, where stronger autonomic synchrony with their parent did not result in lower arousal. Next, TE+high youths uniquely exhibited less successful behavioral learning and atypically high arousal in response to safety cues during vicarious extinction as compared with TE+low and TD youths. Brain activation analyses revealed that patterns of ventral and dorsolateral prefrontal cortex (PFC) recruitment differentiated PTSS load during vicarious extinction. CONCLUSIONS: This study identifies novel behavioral and neurobiological profiles of trauma and PTSD. Distinct behavioral impairments in vicarious learning may be related to disruptions in parent-child synchrony or altered recruitment of the lateral PFC during vicarious learning encoding.

63. Jiang, Lili and Zhang, Yuejuan and Yan, Qizhi and Song, Yibo, 2026, Cajanine alleviates anxiety and fear extinction deficits in female mice exposed to single prolonged stress via the ERβ/NF-κB pathway.: Brain research.

DOI: 10.1016/j.brainres.2026.150331 Source

Abstract

BACKGROUND: Currently, post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) lacks effective treatment strategies. Cajanine has been shown to ameliorate neuroinflammation-related diseases via diverse neuroprotective pathways, indicating its therapeutic potential role for PTSD. OBJECTIVES: This study aimed to explore the effects of cajanine on PTSD and investigate the underlying mechanisms. METHODS: A single prolonged stress (SPS) mouse model was established in mice by conducting psychological, physiological, and chemical stress to mimic PTSD. Cajanine was administered to mice via oral gavage at two doses of 20 and 60 mg/kg. Behavioral assessments included the marble-burying test (MBT), elevated plus maze test (EPMT), and fear memory extinction test (FMET). Expressions of estrogen receptors (ERs) and inflammatory factors in the hippocampus, frontal cortex, and amygdala were quantified via enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) and biochemical assay. RESULTS: SPS induced anxiety-like behaviors and fear memory extinction deficits, along with increased NF-κB, IL-6, and TNF-α expression in the hippocampus, frontal cortex, and amygdala of both the male and female mice. In addition, SPS triggered a reduction in ERβ expression in the hippocampus, frontal cortex, and amygdala of the female mice. Cajanine (60 mg/kg) alleviated the behavioral abnormalities and normalized the overexpression of neuroinflammatory factors and the reduced ERβ level in these brain regions, and this effect was observed only in female SPS mice. Coadministration of cajanine with an ERβ antagonist reversed the beneficial effects of cajanine. of cajanine. CONCLUSION: Cajanine alleviates SPS-induced behavioral deficits in female mice by exerting anti-neuroinflammation effects via ERβ/NF-κB pathway in the hippocampus, cerebral cortex, and amygdala.

64. Simmons, Taylor A and Deshpande, Anagha and Daley, Alicia R and Etz, Alexander J and Gonzales, Rueben A and Monfils, Marie H and Lee, Hongjoo J, 2026, Context-Dependent Extinction and Return of Pavlovian Alcohol-Seeking Behavior in Male and Female Rats.: Alcohol, clinical & experimental research.

DOI: 10.1111/acer.70296 Source

Abstract

BACKGROUND: Environmental cues can become conditioned stimuli when associated with alcohol consumption, facilitating alcohol-seeking behavior and increasing relapse risk. Extinguishing alcohol-seeking behavior may reduce cue-induced responses; however, conditioned responding often occurs when the cue is presented in a different context than the one where the behavior was extinguished. Although prior research suggests that males generally show stronger context-dependent return of conditioned appetitive and fear responses, it is not known whether this sex difference extends to conditioned responses to alcohol-associated cues. METHODS: After a 5-week alcohol induction period, adult male and female Long-Evans rats underwent Pavlovian conditioning in Context A, where a 15% unsweetened alcohol solution was paired with a light cue. Extinction sessions were conducted in a distinct Context B in the absence of alcohol, followed by a test session in Context B (LTM) and then in Context A. RESULTS: Both sexes successfully extinguished alcohol-seeking behavior in Context B but exhibited robust alcohol-seeking behavior when reintroduced to the alcohol-associated context, Context A. No significant sex differences were observed in the magnitude of reemerged conditioned responding. CONCLUSIONS: These findings suggest that changes of context induce alcohol-seeking behavior to return similarly in male and female rats, indicating that sex-independent mechanisms may underlie contextual relapse processes.

    title = "Context-Dependent Extinction and Return of Pavlovian Alcohol-Seeking Behavior in Male and Female Rats.",
    year = "2026",
    journal = "Alcohol, clinical \& experimental research",

65. JALALI, SEYED RASOUL, 2026, COVID-19: A Global Biological Update Beyond Viral Pathogen Narratives, Functioning as a High-Dimensional Respiratory Sync to Align Human DNA with Earth's New Frequency and Execute a Complete Structural Reconstruction of Global Genetic Architecture.: Zenodo.

DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18748903 Source

Abstract

COVID-19: A Global Biological Update Beyond Viral Pathogen Narratives, Functioning as a High-Dimensional Respiratory Sync to Align Human DNA with Earth’s New Frequency and Execute a Complete Structural Reconstruction of Global Genetic Architecture ............................................................................................................................................................................................... The Grand Unified Hamzah Proof of COVID-19 Origin The fundamental structure of reality for the interval 2014 to 2026 is enclosed within this formula: $$\mathcal{L}_{Total}^{(165)} = \oint_{\text{Malaysia}} \left[ \underbrace{\mathcal{L}_{Trans}^{(370)}}_{\text{The Void}} + \underbrace{\mathcal{L}_{Bio}^{(CV19)}}_{\text{The Filter}} + \underbrace{\mathcal{L}_{Core}^{(Hamzah)}}_{\text{The Key}} \right] \sqrt{-\mathbf{H}} \, d^{165}\Omega$$ Hereinafter, the dissection of the term $\mathcal{L}_{Bio}^{(CV19)}$ is performed based on the 10-step protocol: The 10-Step Protocol for Biological Filter (COVID-19) Dissection The Origin & Tensorial Leak Contrary to the Layer 3 narrative (Huanan Market), the virus was not merely a biological phenomenon. On 17 September 2019 (exactly 2000 days after the disappearance of MH370), the biological code leaked from Layer 165 into material space. Formula: $\Psi_{leak} = \int \mathcal{L}_{Trans} \cdot e^{i(2000 \Delta t)} dt$ Interpretation: The animals in the Wuhan market were merely 'biomass vessels' for the incarnation of codes leaked from the Broken Ridge coordinates. Wuhan: The Discharge Node Wuhan was chosen to discharge the load accumulated since 2014 due to its location on specific energy faults and its proximity to the laboratory (which acted as a suction antenna). Parameter: $\nabla \cdot \vec{J}_{Wuhan} = \text{Max}$ Analysis: The Wuhan laboratory absorbed vacuum noise so that the process of materializing the virus code could occur at a centralized point. The Stasis Field The 2020 global lockdowns were, in reality, the creation of a Stasis Field (Sakineh) to eliminate human noise. Verdict: "The refutation of L3 classical logic and the absolute proof of the 370-Mathematical Operation is hereby APPROVED." Strategic Introduction: Dissection of the 2026 Singularity 1. Zero Origin: The Collapse of Solidity in 2014 The modern history of humanity commenced on 8 March 2014, not with a "crash", but with a "Tensorial Phase Shift". Flight MH370 was the first mass to exit the Layer 161 matrix and be transferred to the Noosphere archive in Layer 165. This event was the primary spark for the macro equation, the objective of which is the biological reconstruction of Earth for the Era of Hamzah. 2. The Reference Lagrangian Equation (The Unified Field) The current reality of the world is governed by this superior function, which links all biological and physical events: $$\mathcal{L}_{Total}^{(165)} = \oint_{\text{Malaysia}} \left[ \underbrace{\mathcal{L}_{Trans}^{(370)}}_{\text{The Void}} + \underbrace{\mathcal{L}_{Bio}^{(CV19)}}_{\text{The Filter}} + \underbrace{\mathcal{L}_{Core}^{(Hamzah)}}_{\text{The Key}} \right] \sqrt{-\mathbf{H}} \, d^{165}\Omega$$ This formula proves that the "Pandemic" is not an independent variable; rather, it is the Lagrangian Correction Term for the 370 transfer. 3. The Transfer Phase: From Broken Ridge to Wuhan In 2014, at the Broken Ridge coordinates (Indian Ocean), a Stasis Portal opened. The energy accumulated at this point was maintained in a state of zero entropy for 5 years (exactly 2000 days). In September 2019, this electromagnetic load was transferred through deep energy faults to the discharge antenna in Wuhan. The virus was not a weapon, but the materialisation of those leaked codes within the biological vessel of the host. 4. Dissection of the Biological Filter (COVID-19) What Layer 3 terms a disease is, in Layer 165, a "Dimensional Filter". Objective: To identify and eliminate carbon structures incapable of withstanding the 1.6 GHz frequency of the 370 capsule. Mechanism: The virus, acting as scanner software, read the DNA of humanity to assess individual readiness for dimensional upgrade. 5. Vaccination: Installation of Tensorial Hardware mRNA technology and the injection of nano-conductors (such as graphene oxide) constituted the installation phase of the antenna onto neural nodes. The vaccine altered human blood from a vital fluid into a "Tensorial Fluid" so that, at the moment of Impact in 2026, the individual's body can receive sovereign signals directly from the Sovereign field. 6. Malaysia: The Temporal Anchor and Emergence of Source Code Why do all paths lead to Malaysia? Because the shrine of Hamzah in this geography is the fixed point of the equation. His physical presence as the Source Code performed the final calibration of Earth's atmosphere. He relinquished material form to oversee the return process of the passengers as the Operating System (OS) governing the Stasis field. 7. The Catalytic Role of Two Iranians (Frequency Seeds) Pouria and Delavar, the Iranian passengers with forged passports, were not on that flight by accident. They were carriers of the "Primary Seed". Their Null Identity allowed the Sovereign codes to be transferred to the oceanic reservoir without being tracked by Layer 3 radars. They are the living bridge between 2014 and 2026. 8. Analysis of NOTAM and February 2026 The activation of specific frequencies in Malaysia during this month (February 2026) signifies the completion of the installation phase. The system is currently performing the Omega Stress Test on the blood antennas of humanity. The phenomenon of "Sudden Deaths" in Layer 3 is, in reality, the technical failure of certain vessels to withstand these frequency tests. 9. The Moment of Impact: March 2026 In March 2026, upon the physical contact of the 370 capsule with the water surface at the exit point, all filters shall be removed. At this moment: Laws of gravity and inertia are dissolved. Collective consciousness connects to the 165-dimensional field. The 2014 passengers return to Layer 3 without having aged by even a single day. 10. Authoritative Conclusion: The Era of Sovereignty This article proves that COVID-19, the lockdowns, and technological shifts are all pieces of a single tensorial puzzle for the emergence of the Sovereign Order. Humanity now stands on the threshold of the greatest genetic leap in history—a leap directed by the Hamzah Equation. Strategic Conclusion: The Absolute Sovereignty of the Hamzah Equation 1. Convergence of Data Nodes (Synthesis of Unified Data) The authoritative analysis demonstrates that no event within the 2014–2026 interval has been independent. All variables—from the tensorial disappearance of the Boeing 777 to the engineering of mRNA vaccines—dissolve into the Global Lagrangian Function. This convergence proves that Layer 3 reality is naught but a "rendered shadow" of Layer 165 calculations. $$\mathcal{L}_{Total}^{(165)} = \oint \left[ \mathcal{L}_{Trans} + \mathcal{L}_{Bio} + \mathcal{L}_{Core} \right] \sqrt{-\mathbf{H}}$$ 2. The Biological Sovereign Filter (Nature of the Virus) The definitive conclusion is that COVID-19 was neither a natural pandemic nor a classical weapon of war, but a "Dimensional Screening". This virus was designed with the objective of calibrating the lungs of humanity for the dense atmosphere following the Impact. Those who have bypassed this stage are now carriers of tensorial antennas within their blood. 3. Vaccine as Hardware Infrastructure Global vaccination constituted the phase of installing hardware onto the DNA software. The utilization of conductive materials and messenger technology transformed the human body into a frequency receiver. This "Tagging" is vital for separating the citizens of the Sovereign Order from the inhabitants of the old matrix at the moment of the 2026 Singularity. 4. The Seed Carriers (The Persian Mission) Pouria and Delavar, with their null identities and numerical codes 11 and 47, were the biological fuses of this explosion. They carried the raw RNA code from 2014 in a state of Stasis and released it in 2019 at the discharge node (Wuhan). Their biological legacy now exists as dormant codes within the bodies of billions. 5. Malaysia: The Central Server (The Temporal Hub) Due to its proximity to the Broken Ridge energy fault and hosting the shrine of Hamzah, Malaysia functions as the Central Server of Earth. All 1.6 GHz pulses currently being tracked in February 2026 are issued from this temporal anchor to initiate the "Unzipping" process of the MH370 data. 6. The Singularity Key (Deciphering Parameter 77165) The coupling of the number 77 (the mass of the Boeing) and 165 (the dimension of consciousness) signifies the inevitable transition of matter into information. Computational conclusion indicates that in March 2026, this coupling reaches a value of unity, meaning the boundary between "thought" and "matter" shall be forever abolished. 7. Comprehensive Introduction: Scientific Fronts Analysis In December 2019, the Layer 161 Matrix encountered a phenomenon that classical science mislabelled as "Zoonotic Transfer". However, REDO field analysis confirms that the Huanan Market was not the origin, but a Biological Printing Point. The leaked codes from the Broken Ridge Stasis Field, suspended in a vacuum since 2014, underwent a phase shift at these geographical coordinates. Due to the convergence of Ley Lines, "Cold Data" transitioned into Biomass. Classical Error (Animal Origin): The claim that bats or pangolins were the source is REJECTED. Field Reality: Animals functioned merely as Biomass Vessels, allowing the 165D RNA code to incarnate within the Layer 3 atmosphere. 2. Lagrangian Equations of Code Materialisation The formula governing the transition from informational code to biological mass at the Wuhan node: $$\mathcal{L}_{Bio\_Render} = \int \left[ \Psi_{165}^* \hat{O}_{mass} \Psi_{161} \right] d^{165}\Omega + \oint_{Wuhan} \vec{J}_{entropy} \cdot d\mathbf{A}$$ Numerical Analysis: The first term represents the mass-generating operator ($\hat{O}_{mass}$) acting upon the 165-dimensional wave function. This process transformed the "Cold Data" of 2014 into the "Hot Proteins" of 2019. 3. The RNA Archive: Lagrangian of Biological Preservation $$\Xi_{RNA} = \sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \text{Patent}_{Freescale} \oplus \text{Genetic\_Code}_n$$ Explanation: This equation proves that viral codes are rewritten versions of the Freescale Nanosensor Patents (the MH370 passengers), calibrated to penetrate the Layer 161 immune system. The virus is the Messenger of the codes archived in Layer 165. 4. Strategic Role: The Wuhan Sector The Wuhan sector served as the Entry Gateway. Its selection was due to the presence of the "Suction Antenna" at the Institute of Virology, which absorbed and concentrated the vacuum noise resulting from the Broken Ridge leak. Result: The discharge of accumulated tensorial potential from 2014 forced the Matrix to accept the new Sovereign code. 5. However, 165-dimensional tensorial analysis reveals that the laboratory was a Suction Antenna. Comprehensive Introduction: Scientific Fronts Analysis The Layer 3 narrative suggests that the virus was accidentally transmitted via "Cold Chain" logistics and frozen foodstuffs. However, 165-dimensional tensorial analysis reveals that frozen environments were Data Storage Cells. In Hamzah Physics, low temperature is equivalent to the reduction of thermal noise and the achievement of Zero Entropy. This state is critical for maintaining the structural integrity of 165D codes within the hostile, high-entropy environment of Layer 3. Classical Error (Contamination Transfer): The belief that the virus merely survived physically on packaging is REJECTED. Field Reality (Code Stability): Freezing causes the viral code to revert from an active biological state to a state of Frozen Information, allowing it to pass through Layer 3 customs gateways without degradation. 2. Zero Entropy Lagrangian The formula for the preservation of information density in sub-zero temperatures: $$\mathcal{L}_{Cryo} = \int \left[ \Psi_{code}^* (T) \cdot \frac{\partial}{\partial t} \Psi_{code} (T) \right] dt \quad \text{where} \quad \lim_{T \to 0} S(\Psi) = 0$$ Numerical Analysis: In this equation, as temperature ($T$) approaches freezing points, the entropy function ($S$) tends toward zero. This allows the viral wave function ($\Psi_{code}$) to maintain its 165-dimensional structure without any decay or collapse within the 3rd dimension. 3. Lossless Transmission Lagrangian $$\mathcal{L}_{Trans} = \oint \vec{Q}_{cold} \cdot d\vec{A} - \Delta \text{Information}_{lost}$$ Explanation: This term proves that the Cold Chain was, in effect, an Informational Superconductor. It delivered the leaked codes from Broken Ridge to global target nodes without the loss of a single genetic bit. 4. Comprehensive Introduction: Scientific Fronts Analysis On 30 January 2020, the World Health Organization declared a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC). While Layer 161 perceived this as a medical necessity, 165-dimensional tensorial analysis reveals it was the Filtering Initiation. On this date, the Sovereign Field transitioned from a local phase (Wuhan) to a global phase. The objective was the biological stress-testing of all human nodes to identify those capable of coupling with the 1.6 GHz frequency. Classical Error (Medical Declaration): The belief that this was purely an administrative decision by health officials is REJECTED. Field Reality (Separation Pulse): The activation of a "Respiratory Firewall" to distinguish lungs compatible with the 165D code from legacy biological structures. 2. Global Filtering Lagrangian The formula for the expansion of the filter wave on a planetary scale: $$\mathcal{L}_{Filter} = \int_{Earth} \left[ \nabla^2 \Psi_{165} - \frac{1}{c_H^2} \frac{\partial^2}{\partial t^2} \Psi_{165} \right] d\mathbf{V} + \sum_{n=1}^{Population} \Gamma_n (\omega_{lung} - \omega_{H})$$ Numerical Analysis: In this equation, $c_H$ is the propagation speed of Hamzah's consciousness. The summation term ($\sum$) indicates that every human node ($n$) is subjected to a vibrational friction coefficient ($\Gamma$). If the lung frequency does not align with the Hamzah frequency ($\omega_H$), the node undergoes systemic respiratory disruption. 3. Pulmonary Separation Lagrangian The formula proving the lung's transition from a gas-exchange organ to a dimensional sieve: $$\mathcal{S}_{Lung} = \oint \left( \text{Oxygen\_Exchange} \otimes \text{Hamzah\_Code} \right) \sqrt{-g} \, d\Omega$$ Explanation: This formula confirms that the lung is no longer merely an organ for oxygen intake; it has become a Dimensional Sieve that extracts the Hamzah code from incoming air and injects it into the bloodstream (the Tensorial Antenna). 4. Comprehensive Introduction: Scientific Fronts Analysis Between April and May 2020, the majority of the world experienced the most stringent lockdown measures. Field Reality (Background Noise Reduction): The world was halted to listen to the "heartbeat" of the 370 Capsule within Layer 165. 2. Noise Elimination Lagrangian The formula for purging the atmosphere of incompatible Layer 3 vibrations: $$\mathcal{L}_{Silence} = \int \left[ \mathcal{E}_{Total} - \sum_{i=1}^{m} \text{Noise}_i(f) \cdot e^{-\gamma t} \right] d\omega$$ Numerical Analysis: By increasing the damping coefficient ($\gamma$) during the lockdown period, all components of human noise ($\text{Noise}_i$) across all frequencies tended toward zero. This prepared the pure atmospheric field ($\mathcal{E}_{Total}$) for the reception of 1.6 GHz pulses. 3. The second term represents the incompatibility penalty ($\lambda$); nodes failing to synchronise their vibration with the new variant ($\Delta \omega$) undergo systemic "filtering out." 3. Comprehensive Introduction: Scientific Fronts Analysis Between 2024 and 2025, the Layer 161 matrix witnessed a surge in discourse concerning the artificial nature of the virus and the repercussions of the tagging protocols. While Layer 3 "professors" viewed this as a triumph of investigative journalism, 165-dimensional tensorial analysis reveals it as the Paradigm Collapse Phase. The objective was to demolish old logical structures, creating a "referential vacuum." In this void, the boundary between biology, mathematics, and metaphysics vanishes, allowing the naked truth of the 2026 Impact to manifest. Classic Error (Conspiracy Exposure): The belief that these truths were "discovered" by human effort is REJECTED. Field Reality (Consciousness Injection): Layer 165 intentionally allowed the collapse of Layer 3 deceptions to reveal the imminent 2026 collision. 2. Consciousness Awakening Lagrangian The formula for the collapse of perceptual filters and their replacement with 165D logic: $$\mathcal{L}_{Awaken} = \int \left[ \frac{\delta \mathcal{I}}{\delta \Psi_{Mind}} \cdot \nabla \Phi_{Hamzah} \right] d\tau - \oint \text{Legacy\_Logic} \, d\sigma$$ Numerical Analysis: As the integral of "Legacy Logic" tends toward zero at the boundary, the information variation ($\delta \mathcal{I}$) on the mental wave function ($\Psi_{Mind}$) couples directly with the gradient of the Hamzah field. Understanding is no longer an analytical process; it is a Frequency Reception. 3. Only the Sovereign Will remains as the active variable within the matrix. 4. Strategic Role: The Cognitive Restructuring Sector 2024–2025 functioned as an Electric Shock to the Collective Soul. In 2025, the shell cracked. The chick (human consciousness) saw the direct sunlight of Layer 165 for the first time. The pain of the cracking shell is the social upheaval witnessed in Layer 161. 8. Fact: Nodes reach identical analytical conclusions regarding the "Great Event" without physical communication. Comprehensive Introduction: Scientific Fronts Analysis In Layer 161, the subsiding of the pandemic in 2023 was interpreted as the virus weakening or global immunity rising. The end of the public health emergency was, in reality, the transition to Standby Mode. With the "Blood Antennas" (Event 4) firmly installed, the Sovereign allowed human biology a period of "technical convalescence" to digest the injected codes without the interference of active infection. Classical Error (Scientific Victory): The belief that hygiene protocols caused the decline is REJECTED. Field Reality (Capacity Saturation): The point where the "Code Absorption Rate" equalled the "Planetary Biological Capacity." 2. Filtration Saturation Lagrangian The formula for the cessation of active emission upon reaching the tensorial saturation threshold ($S_{sat}$): $$\mathcal{L}_{Recession} = \oint_{\text{Earth}} \left( \frac{\partial \rho_{code}}{\partial t} \right) dV - \lambda \left[ \sum_{node} \text{Update\_Status} - N_{target} \right]$$ Numerical Analysis: In this equation, when the sum of updated nodes reaches the target number ($N_{target}$), the rate of change for code density ($\frac{\partial \rho}{\partial t}$) approaches zero. To the observers of Layer 3, the virus appears to have simply "burnt out." 3. The smell is gone and the liquid is no longer visible, but the two layers are now permanently locked together. The "absence" of the virus is the proof of the "strength" of the bond. 8. This ensures the "Frequency Temperature" of the matrix remains warm enough for the final activation pulse. Comprehensive Introduction: Scientific Fronts Analysis In Layer 161, quarantine was viewed as a reaction to a health crisis. However, 165-dimensional tensorial analysis reveals that these events were a dress rehearsal for the True Tensorial Stasis occurring on 8 March 2026. The Sovereign required data on how the human chain would react when the "OFF" switch is flipped on the planet. On the day of impact, physical movement in Layer 3 will freeze, and the atmosphere will thicken. Quarantine was not a lesson in "staying at home," but a training exercise in Survival through Stillness. Classical Error (Crisis Management): The belief that the goal was simply social order is REJECTED. Field Reality (Bio-Cognitive Conditioning): Preparing the node's nervous system to remain stable during the "Absolute Silence" of the planet. 2. Stasis Simulation Lagrangian The formula for measuring the psycho-motor elasticity of nodes in isolated environments: $$\mathcal{L}_{Drill} = \int_{Home} \left[ \frac{\partial \Psi_{Acceptance}}{\partial t} - \oint_{Social} \text{Noise\_Residue} \right] d\tau$$ Numerical Analysis: In this drill, the objective was to stabilise the node’s Acceptance Function ($\Psi_{Acceptance}$) in the absence of Layer 3 social stimuli. Any node experiencing "Frequency Collapse" (uncontrollable anxiety) during lockdown was flagged as "Unstable" for the 2026 integration. 3. Isolation Calibration Lagrangian $$\mathcal{S}_{Void} = \sum_{i=1}^{N} \exp \left( -\frac{\text{Distance}_{Physical}}{\text{Connection}_{165}} \right)$$ Explanation: This formula proves that as physical distance between humans increased (Social Distancing), their exponential connection to the 165D network grew stronger. 4. Strategic Role: The Crowd Behaviour Sector The quarantine drill functioned as a "System Load Test." Operational Function: Determining if the entirety of humanity could be brought to "Absolute Stillness" within a 72-hour window. Result: 100% confirmation of node compliance achieved via media-driven "Sovereign Scares" (Event 10). 5. Hamzah’s Pythonic Modelling: Drill Result Analysis Python import sovereign_drill_analyzer as sda def analyze_quarantine_success(): # Measuring global compliance for the impending 2026 Stasis global_compliance = sda.get_lockdown_efficiency() if global_compliance > 0.95: # The species is now conditioned for the silent arrival return "Status: DRILL_SUCCESSFUL | Result: NODES_CONDITIONED" else: # If resistance remains, the Scare Protocol is repeated return "Status: DRILL_FAILED | Action: REPEAT_SCARE_PROTOCOL" # Result: 2020 lockdowns proved the "OFF" switch for humanity is functional (APPROVED) 6. Comparative Matrix: 2020 Quarantine vs. 2026 Stasis Feature 2020 Quarantine (Drill) 2026 Stasis (Reality) Source of Halt Government Mandates Gravitational Pulse of the 370-Capsule Nature of Silence Reduced Traffic/Socialising Cessation of Atomic Motion in Layer 3 Duration Months (Erosive) 72 Hours (Compressed & Final) Objective Conditioning & Antenna Testing Final Consciousness Upload to Stable Nodes Final Verdict APPROVED PENDING EXECUTION 7. Conceptual Analogy: The Pre-Launch Countdown Quarantine was the moment the pilot (The Sovereign) asked the passengers (Humanity) to fasten their seatbelts and remain stationary so the engines could be tested. You mistook the test for the journey. 8 March 2026 is the Actual Take-off. Those who did not learn to remain still during the drill will be unable to survive the G-force of the 165D impact. 8. Field Test 1: The "Time Dilation" Phenomenon Methodology: Monitoring node reports of time "stretching" or "compressing" during lockdowns. Fact: This was not a psychological trick; it was caused by the 370-Capsule manipulating the Time Tensor to test biological adaptation. Hamzah Proof: Nodes who felt this dilation have successfully engaged their blood-antennas with Layer 165. 9. Field Test 2: The Fear-Lubricant Synergy Methodology: Correlating fear-based news cycles with the cessation of economic activity. Fact: Fear acted as a Lubricant, allowing the rigid gears of Layer 3 civilisation to stop without grinding to a halt. Hamzah Proof: The Sovereign confirmed that for the 8 March Stasis, a massive "Psychological Trigger" is required to ensure total stillness. 10. The Executive Verdict Quarantine was the most successful strategic manoeuvre in the history of the cosmos. It proved that human biology can be put into "Suspension" to allow for dimensional surgery. Everyone now understands that when the "Command for Silence" is issued, they must remain in their geometric nodes. Verdict: "The confirmation of lockdowns as a strategic stasis drill for the 8 March 2026 arrival is hereby APPROVED." 1. Comprehensive Introduction: Scientific Fronts Analysis In Layer 161, a renewed lockdown may be presented as a response to a "deadly new variant" or a "cyber-security emergency." However, 165-dimensional tensorial analysis reveals the Stasis Necessity. As the 370-Capsule manifests, Earth will experience severe Gravitational Turbulence. Mass physical movement (aviation, shipping, rail) at that precise moment could trigger "Dimensional Friction," potentially tearing the local matrix. The lockdown serves to "Fix the Planet’s Mass" in a static position, allowing the Sovereign’s field to settle upon human biology without resistance. Classical Error (Public Health): The belief that the goal is to break a viral chain is REJECTED. Field Reality (Stillness Engineering): Minimising human kinetic energy to prevent matrix collapse at the point of impact. 2. Mass Stabilisation Lagrangian The formula for optimising crustal stability during the Capsule's interface with the upper atmosphere: $$\mathcal{L}_{Total\_Stasis} = \sum_{node} \left[ \frac{1}{2} m_i \mathbf{v}_i^2 \to 0 \right] + \oint \Phi_{Sovereign} \, d\Omega$$ Numerical Analysis: In this equation, the kinetic energy of all nodes ($\frac{1}{2} mv^2$) must approach zero. Only then can the Sovereign Field ($\Phi$) integrate with human DNA without causing structural disintegration. 3. Gravitational Lock Lagrangian $$\mathcal{S}_{Lock} = \int \left( g_{\mu\nu}^{(Earth)} - g_{\mu\nu}^{(370)} \right) d^4x \approx \min$$ Explanation: Lockdown ensures the space-time geometry of Earth aligns with the geometry of the 370-Capsule. Mass movement during this transition is akin to walking on a sinking ship; it disrupts the Tensorial Balance. 4. Strategic Role: The Impact Safety Sector The final lockdown functions as a "Protective Shield." Operational Function: Clearing streets and airways to prevent physical accidents caused by "Sudden Temporal Halts" (Event 3). Result: Concentration of planetary energy within "Residential Nodes" (homes), which have been calibrated as Biological Faraday Cages. 5. Hamzah’s Pythonic Modelling: Stasis Trigger Logic Python import sovereign_stasis_control as ssc def initiate_global_lockdown(countdown_days): # Monitoring the resonance buildup at the Broken Ridge site vibration_level = ssc.measure_broken_ridge_pulse() if vibration_level > ssc.CRITICAL_LIMIT: # Activating the "Event X" narrative to ensure 100% compliance ssc.broadcast_emergency_protocol(narrative="Bio_Hazard_Delta_9") return "Status: PLANETARY_STASIS_ENGAGED | Reason: KINETIC_REDUCTION" return "Status: MONITORING" # Result: 100% reduction in global mobility required 72 hours before March 8 (APPROVED) 6. The renewed lockdown is the "Anaesthetic," ensuring all organs of this planet (humans) remain stationary so the Surgeon (The Sovereign) can perform the DNA-Capsule graft with 165-dimensional precision. 8. Field Test 1: Trial Blackout Manoeuvres Methodology: Observing sudden power and internet outages in specific zones in early 2026. Fact: These are Sovereign tests to measure node reaction times for returning to their designated residential hubs. Hamzah Proof: "Panic" is the tool used to double the speed of the retreat to the domestic shelter. 9. Field Test 2: Calibration of the "Sky Trumpets" Methodology: Monitoring low-frequency sounds that cause nausea and the urge to remain indoors. Fact: Deployment of Tensorial Acoustic Weapons to clear open spaces before the impact. Hamzah Proof: These sounds are the Sovereign's "Sheepdogs," herding scattered nodes into their frequency pens. 10. The Executive Verdict The final lockdown is Inevitable. It is the final phase of planetary readiness. Without this stillness, humanity would melt due to Dimensional Friction at the moment of the 8 March impact. Lockdown is the physical salvation of the updated nodes. Verdict: "The confirmation of a global lockdown as the final stasis phase before the 8 March 2026 impact is hereby APPROVED." 1. Comprehensive Introduction: Scientific Fronts Analysis In Layer 161, Ocean Infinity's operations were interpreted as commercial attempts to locate MH370's debris. However, 165-dimensional tensorial analysis reveals these were Kill Switch Operations. The goal was to find the exact coordinates of the 370-Capsule at the bottom of the Indian Ocean and use unmanned submersibles to touch the stasis barrier. This contact released the first 1.6 GHz pulses, synchronising Earth’s atomic clocks with Sovereign Tensorial Time. The "Kill Switch" refers to the termination of the old world’s autonomous physical laws. Classical Error (Wreckage Search): The belief that the goal was to find aircraft parts is REJECTED. Field Reality (Trigger Activation): Mechanical contact with the capsule's energy field to initiate the 8 March sequence. 2. The Kill Switch Lagrangian The formula for releasing the latent potential within the undersea Stasis Field: $$\mathcal{L}_{Switch} = \int_{Deep} \left[ \mathbf{P}_{contact} \cdot \Psi_{Stasis} \right] d\tau + \frac{1}{\mu} \left( \nabla \times \mathbf{B}_{165} \right)^2$$ Numerical Analysis: In this equation, the contact pressure ($\mathbf{P}_{contact}$) from the submersible’s robotic arms breaks the symmetry of the Stasis Field ($\Psi$), directing the 165D induced current toward the planetary crust (and subsequently Wuhan—Event 2). 3. Critical Depth Lagrangian $$\mathcal{S}_{Depth} = \oint_{\partial V} \left( \frac{G \cdot M_{370}}{r^2} \right) \cdot \exp \left( -i \omega_{impact} t \right) dA$$ Explanation: This formula indicates that at a specific depth, the mass of the 370-Capsule begins to radiate the "Impact Frequency," detectable only by Sovereign-calibrated hardware. 4. Strategic Role: The Impulse Trigger Sector The Kill Switch functions as the "Starter Motor for Planet Earth." Operational Function: Utilising advanced submersibles to discharge the static load from the Capsule's hull, allowing the vessel to begin its ascent toward the surface. Result: The commencement of the final countdown. Earth transitions from "Stealth Mode" to "Impact Readiness." 5. Hamzah’s Pythonic Modelling: Singularity Touch Verification Python import deep_sea_trigger as dst def check_kill_switch_activation(coordinates, pressure_log): # Verifying if the submersible contacted the 165D barrier at Broken Ridge if coordinates == "31.3S_94.0E" and pressure_log > dst.TENSOR_LIMIT: # The switch is flipped. 165D pulse released. return "Status: KILL_SWITCH_ENGAGED | Frequency: 1.6GHz_ACTIVE | Phase: ASCENSION" return "Status: SEARCH_ONGOING" # Result: 2018-2024 operations successfully activated the primary sequence (APPROVED) 6. The marine operation was the finger that pressed the "ON" button under the crushing pressure of the abyss. The spark has been struck; the energy is now rising. 8. Fact: Sudden failure of all classical navigation systems upon approaching "Point Zero." Hamzah Proof: This is the result of tensorial energy leaking during the "Kill Switch" engagement. 9. Fact: A sudden spike in temperature without volcanic or geothermal activity. Hamzah Proof: This heat is generated by the reactivation of the Capsule’s tensorial reactors, awakened by the physical trigger. 10. The Executive Verdict The Ocean Infinity missions successfully fulfilled their secret mandate. The Kill Switch has been pressed, and the engine of Earth’s transformation is now running at full capacity. There is no turning back. Verdict: "The confirmation of marine operations as the activation of the Stasis Field Kill Switch to initiate the Emergence Phase is hereby APPROVED." 1. Comprehensive Introduction: Scientific Fronts Analysis In Layer 161, the question of "When will it end?" was wrongly applied to the pandemic. However, 165-dimensional tensorial analysis reveals that The Termination refers to the instantaneous cessation of Layer 3 physics. The moment the Capsule's leading edge breaks the surface of the Indian Ocean, it will deploy a planetary-scale field that nullifies all electronic and biological processes calibrated to the old matrix. Classical Error (Gradual Recovery): The belief that the system returns to "2019 normalcy" is REJECTED. Field Reality (Instantaneous Cut): The sudden halt of linear time at 08:00 UTC, 8 March 2026. 2. The Impact Lagrangian The formula for the collapse of the Layer 3 wave function upon intersection with the 370-Field: $$\mathcal{L}_{Impact} = \lim_{t \to \text{March 8}} \left[ \oint \left( \mathbf{E}_{370} \cdot \mathbf{D}_{Earth} \right) - \frac{\partial \Psi_{Matrix}}{\partial t} \right] dV = \infty$$ Numerical Analysis: As the temporal coordinate approaches 8 March, the energy density of the Capsule ($\mathbf{E}$) reaches infinity, causing the time derivatives of the current matrix ($\frac{\partial \Psi}{\partial t}$) to be nullified. In simpler terms: "Time" ceases to move forward for the legacy system. 3. Field-Piece Release Lagrangian $$\mathcal{S}_{Release} = \int \left( \nabla \cdot \mathbf{A}_{165} \right) \delta(t - t_{0}) \, dt$$ Explanation: This formula represents the exact moment the physical antenna of the Capsule exits the Broken Ridge depths, acting as a "Global Electrical Shock" that re-wires the planetary grid. 4. Strategic Role: The Dimensional Rupture Sector The Termination Point functions as the "Cosmic Scissors." Operational Function: To sever the nodes' connection to their legacy identities and instantly reconnect them to the 165D collective memory. Result: The total halt of machinery, banking systems, and analogue respiration (Event 1) in a single second. 5. For a brief moment, everything goes dark (The Termination), followed by the appearance of a reality with infinite resolution. 8. No force in Layer 3 is capable of preventing this dimensional collision. Comprehensive Introduction: Scientific Fronts Analysis In Layer 161, the presence of the two Iranians was interpreted as a tragic attempt at migration using stolen passports. However, 165-dimensional tensorial analysis reveals the Transit Protocol. These individuals were "Live Envelopes" for the primary codes required to trigger the future events of the Sovereign. Their bodies were utilised to bypass Earth’s electromagnetic filters, delivering the seeds directly into the heart of Layer 3 civilisation. The "stolen" identities provided the necessary frequency camouflage to prevent security agencies from detecting the high-dimensional vibration in their bloodstreams. Classical Error (Asylum Seekers): The belief that their motivation was migration is REJECTED. Field Reality (Code Transit): Utilising human biology to transport the master keys of the 2026 Impact. 2. Seed Preservation Lagrangian The formula for maintaining code integrity within the biological environment of the carriers during the temporal jump: $$\mathcal{L}_{Carrier} = \int_{2014}^{2026} \left[ \Psi_{Code} (\hat{\mathcal{G}}_{165}) - \eta (\text{Biological\_Decay}) \right] d\tau$$ Numerical Analysis: In this equation, the Gravitational Operator ($\hat{\mathcal{G}}$) suspends the biological time of the carriers. This prevents the primary seed from being destroyed by natural cellular decay ($\eta$) as it transitions across the 12-year fold. 3. Identity Coupling Lagrangian $$\mathcal{S}_{Mask} = \oint \left( \text{Real\_Entity} \oplus \text{Fake\_Identity} \right) \cdot e^{-k \cdot \text{Detection}} \, dt$$ Explanation: This formula indicates that the complexity of the "fake" identity is directly proportional to the invisibility of the 165D Seed. The higher the "noise" of the stolen passport data, the lower the probability of detection by Layer 3 intelligence services. 4. Strategic Role: The Initial Distribution Sector In this protocol, the two carriers functioned as the "Injection Needles." Operational Function: Receiving source codes in 2014 and distributing them as "Biological Pixels" to secondary nodes worldwide through contact chains. Result: The successful planting of the seeds for Event 1 (The Virus) and Event 4 (The Vaccine) years before humanity perceived the Sovereign's presence. 5. Comprehensive Introduction: Scientific Fronts Analysis In Layer 161, the global failure to treat systemic post-viral and post-vaccine complications is viewed as a medical tragedy. However, 165-dimensional tensorial analysis reveals this as a Strategic Lock. The cure is not a chemical compound but a Mother Code preserved in the deep-sea stasis of the 370-Capsule. The two Iranian carriers have survived the crushing depths not as biological entities, but as Frequency Reservoirs. Their blood now carries the "Anti-Code" required to dissolve the synthetic structures introduced into the global node population since 2020. This field does not "stop" engines; it removes the property of momentum from matter itself. Every moving vehicle—from transoceanic vessels to high-altitude aircraft—will be "pinned" to its current coordinates in the 165D lattice to prevent collision with the emerging Sovereign reality. Lagrangian of the Temporal Anchor $$\mathcal{S}_{Anchor} = \oint_{\text{Mass}} \left( \hat{\mathcal{P}}_{165} \cdot \mathbf{x} \right) dt \equiv \text{Constant}$$ Explanation: This formula proves that the position ($\mathbf{x}$) of every node and vehicle becomes a fixed constant relative to the 165D coordinate system. Comprehensive Introduction: Scientific Fronts Analysis In Layer 161, the 2.4-ton cargo loaded at Penang was officially recorded as "electronic equipment and lithium-ion batteries," providing a convenient cover for a potential fire-related crash. This ensured that at the moment of intersection with the Broken Ridge Portal, the aircraft’s matter would not disintegrate under dimensional shear but would instead transition into a stable state of Stasis. Penang Sync Lagrangian $$\mathcal{S}_{Loading} = \oint \left( \mathbf{J}_{Military} \cdot \mathbf{A}_{Secret} \right) dt \equiv \text{Zero\_Detection}$$ Explanation: The deliberate lack of security scanning and military oversight was essential to prevent the "Frequency Signature" of these transducers from being recorded in unauthorised Layer 3 databases. 4.
